title: Troxan
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Species |image= |name=Troxan |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Purple]], pink |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions=Gills |lifespan= |origin=[[Troxar]] |habitat= |diet= |language=[[Troxan language]] }} {{Quote|Having an attack of conscience, my apprentice?''"
"''No, Master.''"
"''It was their own greed that brought them to you. In their heart of hearts, they always knew what they were getting into.|Darth Sidious and Darth Tyranus, on the Troxans' decision to surrender to the Galactic Republic during the Clone Wars|}} The '''Troxans''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Troxar]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]. They had gills on their necks that fluttered and vibrated to convey emotion. Gill patterns varied from individual to individual, so much so that those olfactory organs were used as identity markers by Troxan security devices, such as [[incendiary packet]]s. At least part of the Troxan government was known as the [[Benevolence of Troxar]], itself a [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucratic]] organization that featured departments such as the [[Bureau of Patriotic Defense]] and posts such as the [[under-palatine for patriotic liaisons]] and the [[first diplomatic legatee]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Troxans sided with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. However, as the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] gained the upper hand against the Troxans and [[Troxar conflict|inflicted]] severe damage on the species' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], the Troxans considered suing for peace. The species sent a delegation to the Republic, but their appeals were rebuffed. They then hatched a new plan: to pretend to surrender only to mount an insurrection once the Republic recalled most of the [[Clone trooper/Legends|troops]] it had sent to subdue the world. However, at a summit on the planet [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]] with the Separatist [[Head of State/Legends|Head of State]] [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]—actually the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Tyranus—the Count planted [[Mind trick/Legends|suggestions]] in the Troxans' minds to keep them fighting—little did they know that [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], the mastermind behind the war and Dooku's [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]], wanted their world to continue fighting so as to drain the resources of the Republic [[Army/Legends|army]]. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|Gill pattern: Not Applicable''"
"''Fingerprint Identification: Negative''"
"''Retinal Scan: Negative''"
"''Current Bearer cannot be identified as the intended recipient of this Bureau of Diplomatic Liaison Incendiary Packet.''"
"''WARNING! CONTENTS WILL PLASMATE ON PACKET RUPTURE!|Troxan incendiary packet security system|}} Troxans were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] who breathed through gills in their throats. Those respiratory organs came in patterns unique to each Troxan, much as fingerprints were unique to individual [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. When a Troxan was calm, the gills were moist, with a [[Color/Legends|pinkish]] hue. However, when the individual was flustered or excited, the gills changed to a purplish color. The Troxan countenance was otherwise characterized by a [[Nose/Legends|nose]]—large and pronounced in some members of the species—cheeks, and purple skin. Troxans had [[Limb/Legends|hands]] with which to manipulate their environment. Some members of the species could grow quite fat, causing their hands to take on a swollen appearance. They were divided into male and female [[Gender/Legends|sexes]]. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|…I was approached by Senator Amidala of Naboo, who asked me to deliver something to you. Let me assure you, we have taken every precaution here, used the most advanced scanning techniques…''"
"''We thought it might be a bomb.''"
"''Or a bug.''"
"''I still think it could be poisoned. Believe me when I say, your safety, of course, has been uppermost…|Troxan delegation, upon delivering a box to Count Dooku|}} The Troxan [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Troxar]], located in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the species had access to the level of technology that was standard on most [[Galaxy/Legends|galactically]] integrated worlds. Their society boasted experts in demolitions, [[Poison/Legends|poisons]], [[Spy/Legends|surveillance]], and other high-tech professions. Troxan security systems worked by identifying gill patterns and [[Eye/Legends|retinal]] scans to distinguish between individuals. One device that relied on that technology was the [[incendiary packet]], a container that plasmated should anyone try to open it other than its intended recipient. The Troxan government was [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucratic]], with at least part of the government known as the [[Benevolence of Troxar]], and with diplomacy carried out by the [[Bureau of Diplomatic Liaison]]. One official employed by that department was the [[under-palatine for patriotic liaisons]], and individual delegations were headed by the [[first diplomatic legatee]]. The Troxan armed forces were supported by the [[Bureau of Patriotic Defense]], which had an [[Under-palatine for the Bureau of Patriotic Defense|under-palatine]]. The Troxans spoke a [[Language/Legends|language]], also called [[Troxan language|Troxan]], for which they also had a written form. Members of the species could also speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. Troxans supplemented their speech with movements of their gills, which were useful in conveying emotions and states of mind. For example, flapping of the gills conveyed befuddlement, while rippling them indicated derision. Fast, violent gill vibrations indicated anger, while unmoving gills showed calmness. Gill color, too, signified emotion: pink showed a peaceful state of mind, while purple conveyed agitation. Troxans also used their cheeks to convey emotion; sucking them in when flustered, for instance. ==History== {{Quote|Surely you'll be sending them to our quadrant.''"
"''Really, we're key to the entire region.''"
"''Of course, you understand our need.''"
"''What other planets have fought as bravely for the cause?|Count Dooku influences the perceptions of the Troxan delegation to make them believe new battle droid reinforcements are on their way|}} [[File:CountDookuFull-SWE.png|thumb|left|upright|Count Dooku subtly manipulated a Troxan delegation into continuing a hopeless fight against the Republic.]] [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Troxans developed advanced technology and established relations with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Sometime after, the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]] sparked the Clone Wars in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' however, the Troxans allied with the Republic's rival in the war, the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. [[Trooper/Legends|Troops]] from the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] [[Troxar conflict|invaded]] Troxar in response. Although Troxan forces resisted them to the extent of their ability, their world was severely damaged, and the tide of the hostilities turned against them. In the face of what they considered inevitable defeat, the Troxans engaged the Republic in two rounds of [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] talks. In the end, the Republic's legislative body, the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], rejected a diplomatic resolution to hostilities with Troxar, which the Troxans took as evidence that the Republic was only interested in using force or threats so far into the war. However, during the latter conference, [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] presented the Troxan [[Under-palatine for patriotic liaisons (Clone Wars)|first diplomatic legatee]] with a black box to deliver to [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], leader of the Separatists. Troxan experts checked the supposed gift for evidence of explosives, [[Listening device/Legends|listening devices]], or poison, but it seemed clean. The Troxans considered surrendering to the Republic under the assumption that the bulk of Republic [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] would be called away, at which time the Troxans could mount an asymmetrical insurgency to continue the fight. The species sent a delegation to meet with Dooku at [[Château Malreaux]] on the planet [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]] to discuss the ploy and to ask for Separatist reinforcements. The members included the under-palatine for patriotic liaisons—the same individual who had served as the first diplomatic legatee in talks with the Republic—his [[Unidentified Troxan assistant|assistant]], and the [[Under-palatine for the Bureau of Patriotic Defense (Clone Wars)|under-palatine for the Bureau of Patriotic Defense]]. Unbeknownst to the Troxan envoys, [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], [[Sith Master/Legends|master]] of Dooku, who was himself secretly a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], considered Troxar an expendable world due to the damage it had sustained; yet he still felt that it had use to the Separatist cause as a location for the Republic to sink resources and personnel by fighting the Troxans. Sidious accordingly instructed Dooku to persuade the Troxans to continue their conflict by assuring them that Separatist reinforcements would arrive within the [[Standard month/Legends|month]]—an empty promise. Dooku did as he was told: he merely mentioned that new [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] were coming from Separatist factories, and then he used the [[Mind trick/Legends|persuasive ability]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to plant ideas that the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] would be sent to the [[Troxar system/Legends|Troxar system]]. Via the Force, Dooku thus suppressed the realizations that continuing to fight would result in the untold slaughter of Troxan lives, instead emphasizing ideas of egotism and heroism in the Troxar delegation. Before the Troxan envoys parted, they presented Dooku with Amidala's gift. Inside, Dooku found a black, wax [[Candle/Legends|candle]], a reminder of his former home at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], where he trained under the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]. On [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], a [[Unidentified Troxan diplomat|Troxan diplomat]] visited the Jedi Temple to deliver an incendiary packet, addressed to Yoda, which contained a [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojector]] with a message inviting Yoda to visit the Count. The Troxan was greeted at the door by a mere [[Youngling/Legends|youngling]], which he took as a slight—he wanted to speak only to a full-fledged Jedi. The boy, a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] named [[Whie Malreaux/Legends|Whie Malreaux]], was soon joined by his superior, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Maks Leem]], who used the Force to calm the Troxan envoy. She swore to deliver the package directly to Yoda, and the Troxan handed it over. He then boarded a [[flier]] and flew away. More than a century later, in [[137 ABY]], the Troxan homeworld fell within territory controlled by [[Darth Krayt]] as part of his [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Sith Empire]]. ==Behind the scenes== Author [[Sean Stewart]] introduced the Troxans in his novel ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'', which was first published by [[Del Rey]] in [[2004]]. Although the species plays only a small part in the story, theirs is a significant role, since the packages the Troxans convey between Republic and Separatist operatives set the stage for the book's main plot, an [[Mission to Vjun (Clone Wars)|encounter]] between Jedi Master Yoda and his former apprentice, Dooku. The incendiary package the Troxans deliver to the Jedi Temple scans for its recipient's gills, fingerprints, and retinal pattern; however, as is intended for Yoda, a member of [[Yoda's species/Legends|another species]], the book leaves it unclear whether Troxans too have fingerprints and retinas. ==Appearances== *''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' {{Ab|Yoda: Dark Rendezvous (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Expansion Region sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Troxans]] }}
